<content>
1: # Memory Search Presentation Contract
2: 
3: Single source of truth for `/memory:search` startup questions, analysis dashboards, and result displays.
4: 
5: Register (COSTAR): these contracts are written objective-first for an automated-pipeline audience — fixed response shapes, no preamble, no conversational framing inside the rendered block. This is the wording style of the contract itself, not a prompt framework imposed on callers.
6: 
7: ## Template Index
8: 
9: Start with the matching render contract before drafting any response:
10: 
11: - Retrieval results: Section 2, `MEMORY:SEARCH "<query>" ... STATUS=OK RESULTS=<count>`.
12: - Empty results: Section 3, trigger fallback and constitutional rows.
13: - Analysis overview: Section 5.
14: - Analysis subcommands: Section 6.
15: - Errors: Section 7.
16: 
17: ## 1. Startup Question Policy
18: 
19: This section applies ONLY when the command's `ARGUMENT RESOLUTION` header reported `ARGS_PRESENT=false`. If `ARGS_PRESENT=true`, skip this section entirely and render the retrieval or analysis result for the resolved `QUERY` — never ask the startup question over a populated query.
20: 
21: When `ARGS_PRESENT=false` (bare invocation), ask one open-ended question:
22: 
23: ```text
24: What would you like to retrieve or analyze?
25: ```
26: 
27: Accept any natural-language answer as the retrieval query. Do not dump the full intent or subcommand menu at startup.
28: 
29: Ask a targeted follow-up only when genuinely ambiguous:
30: 
31: | Ambiguity | Targeted question |
32: | --- | --- |
33: | User asks for `history` without a folder | `Which spec folder should I show learning history for?` |
34: | User asks for `causal` without an id | `Which spec-doc record ID should I trace?` |
35: | User asks for `preflight` or `postflight` without required fields | `Which spec folder and task id should I use?` |
36: | User says `dashboard` or `ablation` | Route directly to analysis mode. |
37: 
38: If the user asks what is available, show the analysis overview in Section 5.
39: 
40: ## 2. Retrieval Result Display
41: 
42: Use compact, parseable output for model dispatch. No option dumps, score bars, long previews, or hidden metadata unless trace was requested.
43: 
44: ```text
45: MEMORY:SEARCH "<query>" intent=<detected_intent> results=<count>
46: --------------------------------------------------
47: <leaf-folder>/
48:   <score>  #<id>  <title>
49:   <score>  #<id>  <title>
50: 
51: <leaf-folder>/
52:   <score>  #<id>  <title>
53: 
54: STATUS=OK RESULTS=<count> INTENT=<detected_intent>
55: ```
56: 
57: Filled example:
58: 
59: ```text
60: MEMORY:SEARCH "deep research remediation handover" intent=find_spec results=3
61: --------------------------------------------------
62: L8-command-adherence/
63:   0.91  #1842  Command adherence remediation handover
64:   0.87  #1819  Dynamic presentation contract findings
65: 
66: 029-deep-research-remediation/
67:   0.74  #1763  Research-based refinement implementation summary
68: 
69: STATUS=OK RESULTS=3 INTENT=find_spec
70: ```
71: 
72: Field mapping:
73: 
74: | Source field | Template slot |
75: | --- | --- |
76: | User query or `memory_context.input` | `"<query>"` |
77: | Detected or explicit intent | `intent=<detected_intent>` and `INTENT=<detected_intent>` |
78: | Returned result count | `results=<count>` and `RESULTS=<count>` |
79: | `result.specFolder` leaf | `<leaf-folder>/` |
80: | `result.score`, `result.similarity`, or normalized relevance score | `<score>` — render as 0–1 similarity with two decimals (`0.79`, never `79.44`; divide percentage-scaled scores by 100) |
81: | `result.id` or `result.memory_id` | `#<id>` |
82: | `result.title` | `<title>` |
83: 
84: Core-slot mandate (all five, every result, every surface):
85: - `"<query>"` query echo
86: - `<score>` similarity
87: - `#<id>`
88: - `<title>`
89: - `STATUS` footer carrying `RESULTS` and `INTENT`
90: 
91: None of the five is optional. Render every one for any non-empty result set; the only omission path is the empty-result fallback in Section 3.
92: 
93: Score mandate — one score, one scale, one name:
94: - The sole ranking metric is `similarity`, on a 0–1 scale, two decimals (`0.79`). It is the only score shown per row.
95: - BANNED in rendered output: `confidence` as a metric name, and any percentage score. Divide a percentage-scaled value by 100 before emitting (`79.44` → `0.79`).
96: - Do not add a second ranking number under any other name, and do not relabel `similarity` as `confidence`.
97: 
98: Surface-parity clause:
99: - The core-slot set and the 0–1 / two-decimal similarity scale are mandatory on every surface: `--command` dispatch, a direct prompt, and natural conversation.
100: - Conversational answers may wrap the block in prose, but the field set, field names, and scale do not change — two surfaces return comparable, diffable rows for the same result.
101: 
102: Verdict render slots:
103: - `requestQuality` and `citationPolicy` are the only sanctioned extras beyond the core slots.
104: - When present, render each as a named trailing field between the scored result block and the `STATUS` footer, so the footer stays the terminal line:
105: 
106: ```text
107: <leaf-folder>/
108:   <score>  #<id>  <title>
109: 
110: requestQuality <value>
111: citationPolicy <value>
112: 
113: STATUS=OK RESULTS=<count> INTENT=<detected_intent>
114: ```
115: 
116: - Use these exact field names, never an unnamed, renamed, or ad-hoc metric, and never folded into the `<score>` slot.
117: - Under the default contract their absence is valid. When `SPECKIT_ENVELOPE_FIDELITY_V1` is set the two fields are conditionally-mandatory render slots, required-when-present: a verdict field the tool response carries MUST appear unaltered in the rendered block. The tool ships a ready-to-paste `data.envelopeRender` fragment carrying these exact two lines, so the render pastes it verbatim instead of transcribing the fields. Re-emit from `data.envelopeRender` any verdict field the tool shipped but the render dropped. The flag is default-OFF, so the legacy absence-is-valid rule stays the default until the grandfather report is clean.
118: 
119: Folder display rules:
120: - Group by spec folder.
121: - Show only the leaf folder name unless two leaves collide.
122: - Each result line shows only score, id, and title.
123: - Include trace/provenance only when requested or needed to explain a degraded result.
124: 
125: Truncation-resilient rendering:
126: - When `meta.tokenBudgetTruncated` is true (or `meta.returnedResultCount` < `meta.originalResultCount`),
127:   the fully-materialized `data.results` array was trimmed to fit the token budget — it is NOT the
128:   true result count. Render every entry from `data.progressiveDisclosure.results` (id + title from
129:   the snippet), not just the 1–2 detailed rows that survived truncation. Use `meta.originalResultCount`
130:   for `results=<count>`.
131: - Constitutional rows are context, not ranked hits: exclude any result whose `isConstitutional` is
132:   true (or `importanceTier` is `constitutional`) from the ranked block. List them, if relevant, under
133:   a separate `Constitutional rules` heading — never as scored result lines.
134: 
135: ## 3. Empty Result Fallback
136: 
137: ```text
138: MEMORY:SEARCH "<query>" intent=<detected_intent> results=0
139: --------------------------------------------------
140: No spec-doc records matched this query via semantic or lexical search.
141: 
142: Trigger-matched spec-doc records
143:   <leaf-folder>/
144:     #<id>  <title>
145: 
146: Constitutional rules
147:   #<id>  <title>
148: 
149: STATUS=OK RESULTS=0 TRIGGERED=<n> CONSTITUTIONAL=<n>
150: ```
151: 
152: After the block, ask at most one clarifying follow-up if the query was broad.
153: 
154: ## 4. Forbidden Vocabulary
155: 
156: Never emit legacy labels that combine trigger wording with the generic noun `memory` or `memories`, and never label constitutional rows with the generic noun `memory` or `memories`.
157: 
158: Required labels:
159: - Trigger fallback rows: `Trigger-matched spec-doc records` or `Trigger-matched spec-doc record`.
160: - Constitutional rows: `Constitutional rules` or `Constitutional rule`.
161: - Generic result-class label: `Spec-doc record`.
162: 
163: Pre-render gate: scan the candidate output case-insensitively for legacy trigger labels and legacy constitutional-result labels, then rewrite them before emitting.
164: 
165: ## 5. Analysis Overview
166: 
167: Show only when requested or when the user selected analysis tools.
168: 
169: ```text
170: MEMORY:SEARCH ANALYSIS
171: --------------------------------------------------
172: Epistemic
173:   preflight <specFolder> <taskId>
174:   postflight <specFolder> <taskId>
175:   history <specFolder>
176: 
177: Causal graph
178:   causal <memoryId>
179:   link <sourceId> <targetId> <relation>
180:   unlink <edgeId>
181:   causal-stats
182: 
183: Evaluation
184:   ablation
185:   dashboard
186: 
187: STATUS=OK ACTION=overview
188: ```
189: 
190: ## 6. Analysis Result Displays
191: 
192: ### Preflight
193: 
194: ```text
195: MEMORY:SEARCH PREFLIGHT
196: --------------------------------------------------
197: Spec        <specFolder>
198: Task        <taskId>
199: Knowledge   <score>/100
200: Uncertainty <score>/100
201: Context     <score>/100
202: 
203: Gaps
204: - <gap>
205: 
206: STATUS=OK ACTION=preflight
207: ```
208: 
209: ### Postflight
210: 
211: ```text
212: MEMORY:SEARCH POSTFLIGHT
213: --------------------------------------------------
214: Spec        <specFolder>
215: Task        <taskId>
216: Knowledge   <pre> -> <post> (delta <delta>)
217: Uncertainty <pre> -> <post> (delta <delta>)
218: Context     <pre> -> <post> (delta <delta>)
219: Learning    <value> (<interpretation>)
220: 
221: STATUS=OK ACTION=postflight LI=<value>
222: ```
223: 
224: ### History
225: 
226: ```text
227: MEMORY:SEARCH HISTORY
228: --------------------------------------------------
229: Spec        <specFolder>
230: Records     <count>
231: 
232: <taskId>  K <pre>-><post>  U <pre>-><post>  C <pre>-><post>  LI <value>
233: 
234: STATUS=OK ACTION=history
235: ```
236: 
237: ### Causal
238: 
239: ```text
240: MEMORY:SEARCH CAUSAL
241: --------------------------------------------------
242: Record      #<id> "<title>"
243: Depth       <maxDepth>
244: Direction   <direction>
245: 
246: Chain
247: - #<id> "<title>" --<relation>--> #<id2> "<title2>"
248: 
249: STATUS=OK ACTION=causal EDGES=<count>
250: ```
251: 
252: ### Link and Unlink
253: 
254: ```text
255: MEMORY:SEARCH LINK
256: --------------------------------------------------
257: Source      #<sourceId>
258: Target      #<targetId>
259: Relation    <relation>
260: 
261: STATUS=OK ACTION=link
262: ```
263: 
264: ```text
265: MEMORY:SEARCH UNLINK
266: --------------------------------------------------
267: Removed     Edge #<edgeId>
268: 
269: STATUS=OK ACTION=unlink
270: ```
271: 
272: ### Causal Stats
273: 
274: ```text
275: MEMORY:SEARCH CAUSAL-STATS
276: --------------------------------------------------
277: Total Edges <count>
278: Coverage    <pct>% (<linked>/<total> spec-doc records)
279: 
280: By relation
281: caused       <count>
282: enabled      <count>
283: supersedes   <count>
284: contradicts  <count>
285: derived_from <count>
286: supports     <count>
287: 
288: STATUS=OK ACTION=causal-stats COVERAGE=<pct>%
289: ```
290: 
291: ### Evaluation
292: 
293: ```text
294: MEMORY:SEARCH ABLATION
295: --------------------------------------------------
296: Recall@<K> deltas
297: -vector   <delta>
298: -bm25     <delta>
299: -fts5     <delta>
300: -graph    <delta>
301: -trigger  <delta>
302: 
303: STATUS=OK ACTION=ablation
304: ```
305: 
306: ```text
307: MEMORY:SEARCH DASHBOARD
308: --------------------------------------------------
309: Sprint trends
310: <sprint>  Recall@20 <value>  MRR <value>
311: 
312: Channel performance
313: vector <value>
314: bm25   <value>
315: fts5   <value>
316: 
317: STATUS=OK ACTION=dashboard
318: ```
319: 
320: ## 7. Error Display
321: 
322: ```text
323: MEMORY:SEARCH
324: --------------------------------------------------
325: FAIL        <short reason>
326: Recovery    <next safe action>
327: 
328: STATUS=FAIL ERROR="<message>"
329: ```

(End of file - total 329 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# Memory Search Presentation Contract

Single source of truth for `/memory:search` startup questions, analysis dashboards, and result displays.

Register (COSTAR): these contracts are written objective-first for an automated-pipeline audience — fixed response shapes, no preamble, no conversational framing inside the rendered block. This is the wording style of the contract itself, not a prompt framework imposed on callers.

## Template Index

Start with the matching render contract before drafting any response:

- Retrieval results: Section 2, `MEMORY:SEARCH "<query>" ... STATUS=OK RESULTS=<count>`.
- Empty results: Section 3, trigger fallback and constitutional rows.
- Analysis overview: Section 5.
- Analysis subcommands: Section 6.
- Errors: Section 7.

## 1. Startup Question Policy

This section applies ONLY when the command's `ARGUMENT RESOLUTION` header reported `ARGS_PRESENT=false`. If `ARGS_PRESENT=true`, skip this section entirely and render the retrieval or analysis result for the resolved `QUERY` — never ask the startup question over a populated query.

display.text:
# Memory Search Presentation Contract

Single source of truth for `/memory:search` startup questions, analysis dashboards, and result displays.

Register (COSTAR): these contracts are written objective-first for an automated-pipeline audience — fixed response shapes, no preamble, no conversational framing inside the rendered block. This is the wording style of the contract itself, not a prompt framework imposed on callers.

## Template Index

Start with the matching render contract before drafting any response:

- Retrieval results: Section 2, `MEMORY:SEARCH "<query>" ... STATUS=OK RESULTS=<count>`.
- Empty results: Section 3, trigger fallback and constitutional rows.
- Analysis overview: Section 5.
- Analysis subcommands: Section 6.
- Errors: Section 7.

## 1. Startup Question Policy

This section applies ONLY when the command's `ARGUMENT RESOLUTION` header reported `ARGS_PRESENT=false`. If `ARGS_PRESENT=true`, skip this section entirely and render the retrieval or analysis result for the resolved `QUERY` — never ask the startup question over a populated query.

When `ARGS_PRESENT=false` (bare invocation), ask one open-ended question:

```text
What would you like to retrieve or analyze?
```

Accept any natural-language answer as the retrieval query. Do not dump the full intent or subcommand menu at startup.

Ask a targeted follow-up only when genuinely ambiguous:

| Ambiguity | Targeted question |
| --- | --- |
| User asks for `history` without a folder | `Which spec folder should I show learning history for?` |
| User asks for `causal` without an id | `Which spec-doc record ID should I trace?` |
| User asks for `preflight` or `postflight` without required fields | `Which spec folder and task id should I use?` |
| User says `dashboard` or `ablation` | Route directly to analysis mode. |

If the user asks what is available, show the analysis overview in Section 5.

## 2. Retrieval Result Display

Use compact, parseable output for model dispatch. No option dumps, score bars, long previews, or hidden metadata unless trace was requested.

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH "<query>" intent=<detected_intent> results=<count>
--------------------------------------------------
<leaf-folder>/
  <score>  #<id>  <title>
  <score>  #<id>  <title>

<leaf-folder>/
  <score>  #<id>  <title>

STATUS=OK RESULTS=<count> INTENT=<detected_intent>
```

Filled example:

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH "deep research remediation handover" intent=find_spec results=3
--------------------------------------------------
L8-command-adherence/
  0.91  #1842  Command adherence remediation handover
  0.87  #1819  Dynamic presentation contract findings

029-deep-research-remediation/
  0.74  #1763  Research-based refinement implementation summary

STATUS=OK RESULTS=3 INTENT=find_spec
```

Field mapping:

| Source field | Template slot |
| --- | --- |
| User query or `memory_context.input` | `"<query>"` |
| Detected or explicit intent | `intent=<detected_intent>` and `INTENT=<detected_intent>` |
| Returned result count | `results=<count>` and `RESULTS=<count>` |
| `result.specFolder` leaf | `<leaf-folder>/` |
| `result.score`, `result.similarity`, or normalized relevance score | `<score>` — render as 0–1 similarity with two decimals (`0.79`, never `79.44`; divide percentage-scaled scores by 100) |
| `result.id` or `result.memory_id` | `#<id>` |
| `result.title` | `<title>` |

Core-slot mandate (all five, every result, every surface):
- `"<query>"` query echo
- `<score>` similarity
- `#<id>`
- `<title>`
- `STATUS` footer carrying `RESULTS` and `INTENT`

None of the five is optional. Render every one for any non-empty result set; the only omission path is the empty-result fallback in Section 3.

Score mandate — one score, one scale, one name:
- The sole ranking metric is `similarity`, on a 0–1 scale, two decimals (`0.79`). It is the only score shown per row.
- BANNED in rendered output: `confidence` as a metric name, and any percentage score. Divide a percentage-scaled value by 100 before emitting (`79.44` → `0.79`).
- Do not add a second ranking number under any other name, and do not relabel `similarity` as `confidence`.

Surface-parity clause:
- The core-slot set and the 0–1 / two-decimal similarity scale are mandatory on every surface: `--command` dispatch, a direct prompt, and natural conversation.
- Conversational answers may wrap the block in prose, but the field set, field names, and scale do not change — two surfaces return comparable, diffable rows for the same result.

Verdict render slots:
- `requestQuality` and `citationPolicy` are the only sanctioned extras beyond the core slots.
- When present, render each as a named trailing field between the scored result block and the `STATUS` footer, so the footer stays the terminal line:

```text
<leaf-folder>/
  <score>  #<id>  <title>

requestQuality <value>
citationPolicy <value>

STATUS=OK RESULTS=<count> INTENT=<detected_intent>
```

- Use these exact field names, never an unnamed, renamed, or ad-hoc metric, and never folded into the `<score>` slot.
- Under the default contract their absence is valid. When `SPECKIT_ENVELOPE_FIDELITY_V1` is set the two fields are conditionally-mandatory render slots, required-when-present: a verdict field the tool response carries MUST appear unaltered in the rendered block. The tool ships a ready-to-paste `data.envelopeRender` fragment carrying these exact two lines, so the render pastes it verbatim instead of transcribing the fields. Re-emit from `data.envelopeRender` any verdict field the tool shipped but the render dropped. The flag is default-OFF, so the legacy absence-is-valid rule stays the default until the grandfather report is clean.

Folder display rules:
- Group by spec folder.
- Show only the leaf folder name unless two leaves collide.
- Each result line shows only score, id, and title.
- Include trace/provenance only when requested or needed to explain a degraded result.

Truncation-resilient rendering:
- When `meta.tokenBudgetTruncated` is true (or `meta.returnedResultCount` < `meta.originalResultCount`),
  the fully-materialized `data.results` array was trimmed to fit the token budget — it is NOT the
  true result count. Render every entry from `data.progressiveDisclosure.results` (id + title from
  the snippet), not just the 1–2 detailed rows that survived truncation. Use `meta.originalResultCount`
  for `results=<count>`.
- Constitutional rows are context, not ranked hits: exclude any result whose `isConstitutional` is
  true (or `importanceTier` is `constitutional`) from the ranked block. List them, if relevant, under
  a separate `Constitutional rules` heading — never as scored result lines.

## 3. Empty Result Fallback

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH "<query>" intent=<detected_intent> results=0
--------------------------------------------------
No spec-doc records matched this query via semantic or lexical search.

Trigger-matched spec-doc records
  <leaf-folder>/
    #<id>  <title>

Constitutional rules
  #<id>  <title>

STATUS=OK RESULTS=0 TRIGGERED=<n> CONSTITUTIONAL=<n>
```

After the block, ask at most one clarifying follow-up if the query was broad.

## 4. Forbidden Vocabulary

Never emit legacy labels that combine trigger wording with the generic noun `memory` or `memories`, and never label constitutional rows with the generic noun `memory` or `memories`.

Required labels:
- Trigger fallback rows: `Trigger-matched spec-doc records` or `Trigger-matched spec-doc record`.
- Constitutional rows: `Constitutional rules` or `Constitutional rule`.
- Generic result-class label: `Spec-doc record`.

Pre-render gate: scan the candidate output case-insensitively for legacy trigger labels and legacy constitutional-result labels, then rewrite them before emitting.

## 5. Analysis Overview

Show only when requested or when the user selected analysis tools.

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH ANALYSIS
--------------------------------------------------
Epistemic
  preflight <specFolder> <taskId>
  postflight <specFolder> <taskId>
  history <specFolder>

Causal graph
  causal <memoryId>
  link <sourceId> <targetId> <relation>
  unlink <edgeId>
  causal-stats

Evaluation
  ablation
  dashboard

STATUS=OK ACTION=overview
```

## 6. Analysis Result Displays

### Preflight

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH PREFLIGHT
--------------------------------------------------
Spec        <specFolder>
Task        <taskId>
Knowledge   <score>/100
Uncertainty <score>/100
Context     <score>/100

Gaps
- <gap>

STATUS=OK ACTION=preflight
```

### Postflight

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH POSTFLIGHT
--------------------------------------------------
Spec        <specFolder>
Task        <taskId>
Knowledge   <pre> -> <post> (delta <delta>)
Uncertainty <pre> -> <post> (delta <delta>)
Context     <pre> -> <post> (delta <delta>)
Learning    <value> (<interpretation>)

STATUS=OK ACTION=postflight LI=<value>
```

### History

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH HISTORY
--------------------------------------------------
Spec        <specFolder>
Records     <count>

<taskId>  K <pre>-><post>  U <pre>-><post>  C <pre>-><post>  LI <value>

STATUS=OK ACTION=history
```

### Causal

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH CAUSAL
--------------------------------------------------
Record      #<id> "<title>"
Depth       <maxDepth>
Direction   <direction>

Chain
- #<id> "<title>" --<relation>--> #<id2> "<title2>"

STATUS=OK ACTION=causal EDGES=<count>
```

### Link and Unlink

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH LINK
--------------------------------------------------
Source      #<sourceId>
Target      #<targetId>
Relation    <relation>

STATUS=OK ACTION=link
```

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH UNLINK
--------------------------------------------------
Removed     Edge #<edgeId>

STATUS=OK ACTION=unlink
```

### Causal Stats

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH CAUSAL-STATS
--------------------------------------------------
Total Edges <count>
Coverage    <pct>% (<linked>/<total> spec-doc records)

By relation
caused       <count>
enabled      <count>
supersedes   <count>
contradicts  <count>
derived_from <count>
supports     <count>

STATUS=OK ACTION=causal-stats COVERAGE=<pct>%
```

### Evaluation

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH ABLATION
--------------------------------------------------
Recall@<K> deltas
-vector   <delta>
-bm25     <delta>
-fts5     <delta>
-graph    <delta>
-trigger  <delta>

STATUS=OK ACTION=ablation
```

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH DASHBOARD
--------------------------------------------------
Sprint trends
<sprint>  Recall@20 <value>  MRR <value>

Channel performance
vector <value>
bm25   <value>
fts5   <value>

STATUS=OK ACTION=dashboard
```

## 7. Error Display

```text
MEMORY:SEARCH
--------------------------------------------------
FAIL        <short reason>
Recovery    <next safe action>

STATUS=FAIL ERROR="<message>"
```
